body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-23T07:13:12</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>2</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>39</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>23/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>23/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>2</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>98</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>-3.1</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>-3.1</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>-1</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate 26-40 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>NW</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>Snow Flurries</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Fair</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Cloudy</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Cloudy. Medium chance of snow showers, becoming less likely this afternoon. Winds west to southwesterly 25 to 35 km/h.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>It Snowed!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>The temperature plummeted in the early hours, and we've picked up a couple of centimeters and the snowguns have fired up.&nbsp; The current temp is minus 3, heading for a top of minus 1 and the wind is a moderate northwesterly which should swing more to the south during the day. There is the odd flurry still coming through this morning.</p><p>The mix of natural and machine-made snow should make for ok conditions for carving a few turns on the groomers this morning but there hasn't been enough just yet to get too adventurous. Off piste will be dust on a very firm crust... and best avoided.&nbsp; &nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley area is open but the cover is thin, the snow is hard and the runs are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders. The extreme area is closed due to a significant ice hazard, and please do not attempt to retrieve dropped equipment from closed areas of the resort.</p><p>Rug up for the windchill and have a beaut one.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Cheers Bill</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>23 July 2026

There is a significant sliding hazard in the backcountry again today, plus the cover is thin, becoming more marginal the lower you go. All aspects will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day. There has not been enough fresh snow to reduce the sliding hazard. Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
